content_en:
What happened in the Battle of Badr, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah advanced, tearing his way into the middle of the enemy ranks. This bold move of his created havoc and the enemy started to run helterskelter. He was going around fearlessly in the middle of the enemy as if death was of no consequence and there was absolutely no danger. His casual attitude frightened the Quraish. As soon as he came face to face with an enemy on horseback, the man would give way to him. But there was one man among them who would stand in front of him to challenge him and try to attack him with his sword. Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . would try to avoid him and retreat. But the man would insist on coming face to face to oppose him, and Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 would always give him free passage and get out of his way. During the battle a stage came when the man had him trapped. There was no way that Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 could turn to avoid him; he could neither move towards the front nor could he move back; all avenues were closed to him. Absolutely ensnared, he was forced to attack him on the head with his sword; his skull broke into two and in a moment he lay dead at his feet. Can you guess who this slain man could be? Doubtless the trial of faithfulness faced by Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah <% was extremely critical, few people

are forced into such a situation. When he realised that the dead man was his father he was stunned.

The fact was that the man he had killed was to all appearances his father, but in reality it was the standard-bearer of evil he had killed. Fuifilling this exemplary role he had made it clear that in the balance it was Allah Almighty and His religion of Islam which outweighed all other considerations and even familial bonds. The love of Allah 3S takes priority and is superior to all other bonds. Every moment the objective is in sight and of paramount importance (the glory of Islam). If something comes in the way it has to be chopped off; if a mountain is an obstacle it has to be surmounted; if there is a river in the way the waves have to be cut through with powerful strokes to reach the friendly shores. The sword of Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah did not chop off his father’s neck, but it chopped off the face of ’Kufr’ and one who associated others with the One True Allah. This exploit of his pleased Allah 38 so much that the following Aydt were revealed:

“You (O Muhammad 38) will not find any peopie who believe in Allah and the Last Day, making friendship with those who oppose Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad 38), even though they were their fathers or their sons or their brothers or their kindred (people). For such He has written Faith in their hearts, and strengthened them with Riih (proofs, light and true guidance) from Himself. And He will admit them to Gardens (Paradise) under which rivers flow, to dwell therein (forever). Allah is pleased with them and they with Him. They are the party of Allah. Verily, it is the party of Allah that will be the successful.” (59:22)

What better fate could one ask for than praise for one’s deeds in the radiant Verses revealed in the Noble Quran? No doubt, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . received this enviable honour. As long as this world

exists the Noble Qur’an will continue to be read, and the Mo’mineen' will give life and spirit to their determination, courage and faith.

' True believers - believers in word and deed.

The fighting was at its most intense in the Battle of Uhud. The sound of the clanging of swords was deafening, there was so much shouting and yelling that one’s hair stood up on end. Both armies were equally matched. And then the enemy began to retreat, and the Muslim forces chased them right up to their tents; the archers too leapt down from their positions. Khalid bin Waleed surrounded them and the rest of the enemies too turned back. At this stage in the battle Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah % was in the front ranks and surrounded, but realising that the Prophet 38 was in danger and breaking through the dangerous encircling enemy cordon, he reached him. The Prophet 38 was lying down bleeding from his injuries and cleaning up the blood from his wounds. He was saying sorrowfully:

“How can those people prosper who have bloodied the face of their Prophet, though all he was doing was to invite them to their Creator and Sustainer?”

“The Prophet 3% was injured in the Battle of Uhud. A couple of links from his own helmet had pierced through his forehead, which was bleeding. I ran tc.vards him, and then suddenly, what should I see but a friend running towards him from the east. He reached him before me, and I saw that it was the selfsacrificing Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . He requested that I should give him the honour of removing the links of the helmet. Then with great care, but at the same time with great force he held on to the Jinks with his front teeth. He pulled out one link, but lost a tooth in the process. Then without bothering about the pain he must be suffering, again he held on to the other link with his teeth and exerted force. As the other link came out, he lost another tooth as well. Thus two of his teeth were sacrificed, but his beloved Prophet # was relieved and found comfort. It seemed as if he looked even more handsome and graceful than before with the loss of his two front teeth, because it was all done for love of his Prophet 38. All the other Companions of the Prophet 3% used to look at him with envious eyes. There the only criterion for judging the worth of a person was the extent to which he was willing to sacrifice himself for the Prophet 38.

in 6 Hijrah, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah -4 was appointed commander of a troop of three hundred men to track down and report on the movements of trading and military caravans of the Quraish. He had to travel along the coastal areas to complete this assignment.

relief; and after every difficulty there is ease. For every difficulty there is a doubled comfort. When they got such a meagre supply of dates for three hundred Mujahideen there was not a word of complaint. They had absolute faith and trust in Allah Almighty and His Messenger. The depth of their belief in Allah Almighty gave them the certitude that the being who had created them would also provide for them; the Almighty Allah for Whose cause they had stepped out into the battlefield would provide ample means of sustenance for them, and He did provide something which had never even entered their minds.

‘Umar Farooq # says:

“T had never deeply wished to occupy any position, but when I heard this sentence repeated by the Prophet 3%, | wished that I should be the object on whom this praise was showered. | hurried for the noon prayers to the mosque. After having led the prayers, the Prophet 3 looked to his left and to his right. I stretched my neck in order to attract his attention. He kept glancing at all the people who had assembled for the prayers. Finally, his eye fell on Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 2. He called him and told him to accompany the delegation and do what was just and right in fulfilling the contract with them.”
content_id:
Apa yang terjadi dalam Perang Badr, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah maju ke depan, menerobos masuk ke tengah barisan musuh. Langkah beraninya ini menimbulkan kekacauan dan musuh mulai berlarian ke segala arah. Ia bergerak tanpa rasa takut di tengah-tengah musuh seolah-olah kematian bukan sesuatu yang berarti dan sama sekali tidak ada bahaya. Sikap tenangnya itu membuat kaum Quraisy ketakutan. Begitu ia berhadapan muka dengan seorang musuh berkuda, orang itu akan menyingkir memberi jalan kepadanya. Namun ada seorang di antara mereka yang selalu berdiri di hadapannya untuk menantangnya dan berusaha menyerangnya dengan pedang. Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah berusaha menghindarinya dan mundur. Tetapi orang itu terus bersikeras menghadangnya, dan Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah selalu memberinya jalan bebas dan menyingkir. Selama pertempuran tibalah suatu tahap di mana orang itu berhasil mengepungnya. Tidak ada jalan bagi Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah untuk mengelak darinya; ia tidak bisa bergerak ke depan maupun ke belakang; semua jalan tertutup baginya. Benar-benar terjebak, ia terpaksa menyerang kepala orang itu dengan pedangnya; tengkoraknya terbelah dua dan dalam sekejap ia tergeletak mati di kakinya. Dapatkah kalian menebak siapakah orang yang terbunuh ini? Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa ujian keimanan yang dihadapi Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah sangatlah berat, sedikit orang yang

dipaksa menghadapi situasi seperti itu. Ketika ia menyadari bahwa orang yang tewas itu adalah ayahnya sendiri, ia terkejut.

Kenyataannya adalah bahwa orang yang ia bunuh secara lahiriah memang ayahnya, tetapi pada hakikatnya ia telah membunuh pembawa panji kejahatan. Dengan memenuhi peran teladan ini, ia telah memperjelas bahwa dalam timbangan keadilan, Allah Yang Maha Kuasa dan agama Islam-Nya lebih berat dari segala pertimbangan lain bahkan ikatan kekeluargaan. Kecintaan kepada Allah lebih utama dan lebih tinggi dari segala ikatan lainnya. Setiap saat tujuan selalu terlihat dan menjadi yang terpenting (kejayaan Islam). Jika sesuatu menghalangi jalan, ia harus ditebas; jika gunung menjadi rintangan, ia harus didaki; jika sungai menghadang, ombaknya harus ditembus dengan kayuhan kuat untuk mencapai tepian yang aman. Pedang Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah tidak memenggal leher ayahnya, melainkan memenggal wajah kekufuran dan orang yang menyekutukan Allah Yang Maha Esa. Perbuatannya ini begitu menyenangkan Allah sehingga ayat-ayat berikut diturunkan:

"Engkau (wahai Muhammad) tidak akan mendapati suatu kaum yang beriman kepada Allah dan hari akhir, saling berkasih sayang dengan orang-orang yang menentang Allah dan Rasul-Nya (Muhammad), sekalipun orang-orang itu adalah bapak-bapak mereka, atau anak-anak mereka, atau saudara-saudara mereka, atau kerabat mereka. Mereka itulah orang-orang yang Allah telah menanamkan keimanan dalam hati mereka dan menguatkan mereka dengan Ruh (bukti-bukti, cahaya dan petunjuk yang benar) dari-Nya. Dan Dia akan memasukkan mereka ke dalam surga-surga yang mengalir di bawahnya sungai-sungai, mereka kekal di dalamnya. Allah ridha terhadap mereka dan mereka pun ridha kepada-Nya. Mereka itulah golongan Allah. Ketahuilah, sesungguhnya golongan Allah itulah yang beruntung." (QS. Al-Mujadilah: 22)

Nasib apa yang lebih baik yang bisa diminta selain pujian atas amal perbuatan seseorang dalam ayat-ayat mulia yang diturunkan dalam Al-Quran? Tidak diragukan lagi, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah menerima kehormatan yang sangat didambakan ini. Selama dunia ini masih ada, Al-Quran akan terus dibaca, dan kaum Mukminin akan menghidupkan dan menyemangati tekad, keberanian dan keimanan mereka.

Mukminin sejati - orang-orang yang beriman dalam ucapan dan perbuatan.

Pertempuran berlangsung sangat sengit dalam Perang Uhud. Suara dentingan pedang yang beradu sangat memekakkan telinga, begitu banyak teriakan dan pekikan sehingga bulu kuduk berdiri. Kedua pasukan sama kuatnya. Dan kemudian musuh mulai mundur, dan pasukan Muslim mengejar mereka hingga ke tenda-tenda mereka; para pemanah pun turut melompat turun dari posisi mereka. Khalid bin Waleed mengepung mereka dan sisa musuh pun berbalik. Pada tahap pertempuran ini Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah berada di barisan depan dan terkepung, namun menyadari bahwa Nabi dalam bahaya, ia menerobos kepungan musuh yang berbahaya dan mencapai beliau. Nabi terbaring berlumuran darah dari luka-lukanya dan membersihkan darah dari lukanya. Beliau berkata dengan sedih:

"Bagaimana mungkin kaum yang telah melukai wajah Nabi mereka akan beruntung, padahal yang ia lakukan hanyalah mengajak mereka kepada Pencipta dan Pemelihara mereka?"

"Nabi terluka dalam Perang Uhud. Beberapa mata rantai dari helmnya sendiri menusuk dahinya hingga berdarah. Aku berlari ke arahnya, dan kemudian tiba-tiba, apa yang kulihat selain seorang sahabat berlari ke arahnya dari timur. Ia mencapai beliau sebelum aku, dan kulihat ternyata itu adalah Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah yang rela berkorban jiwa. Ia meminta agar aku memberinya kehormatan untuk mencabut mata rantai helm itu. Kemudian dengan sangat hati-hati, tetapi pada saat yang sama dengan tenaga yang kuat, ia menggigit mata rantai itu dengan gigi depannya. Ia mencabut satu mata rantai, tetapi kehilangan satu gigi dalam prosesnya. Kemudian tanpa mempedulikan rasa sakit yang pasti dirasakannya, ia kembali menggigit mata rantai yang lain dengan giginya dan mengerahkan tenaga. Ketika mata rantai lainnya tercabut, ia kehilangan satu gigi lagi. Dengan demikian dua giginya berkorban, tetapi Nabi tercintanya merasa lega dan nyaman. Ia tampak bahkan lebih tampan dan anggun dari sebelumnya dengan hilangnya dua gigi depannya, karena semua itu dilakukan demi kecintaan kepada Nabinya. Semua Sahabat Nabi yang lain memandangnya dengan mata penuh iri. Di sana satu-satunya tolok ukur untuk menilai kemuliaan seseorang adalah sejauh mana ia bersedia mengorbankan dirinya demi Nabi.

Pada tahun 6 Hijrah, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah ditunjuk sebagai komandan sebuah pasukan beranggotakan tiga ratus orang untuk melacak dan melaporkan pergerakan kafilah dagang dan militer Quraish. Ia harus menempuh jalur pesisir pantai untuk menyelesaikan tugas ini.

keringanan; dan setelah setiap kesulitan ada kemudahan. Untuk setiap kesulitan ada kenyamanan berlipat ganda. Ketika mereka mendapat jatah kurma yang begitu sedikit untuk tiga ratus Mujahidin, tidak ada sepatah kata keluhan pun. Mereka memiliki keimanan dan tawakkul yang mutlak kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa dan Rasul-Nya. Dalamnya keyakinan mereka kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa memberi mereka kepastian bahwa Dzat yang telah menciptakan mereka juga akan memberi rezeki kepada mereka; Allah Yang Maha Kuasa yang demi-Nya mereka melangkah ke medan perang akan menyediakan sarana penghidupan yang berlimpah bagi mereka, dan Dia memang menyediakan sesuatu yang bahkan tidak pernah terlintas dalam pikiran mereka.

'Umar Farooq berkata:

"Aku tidak pernah sangat berharap untuk menduduki suatu kedudukan, tetapi ketika aku mendengar kalimat ini diulang oleh Nabi, aku berharap akulah orang yang menerima pujian itu. Aku bergegas ke masjid untuk salat zhuhur. Setelah memimpin salat, Nabi menoleh ke kiri dan ke kanan. Aku menjulurkan leherku agar menarik perhatian beliau. Beliau terus memandang ke semua orang yang berkumpul untuk salat. Akhirnya, pandangan beliau jatuh pada Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah. Beliau memanggilnya dan menyuruhnya menemani delegasi itu serta bertindak adil dan benar dalam memenuhi perjanjian dengan mereka."
content_ar: What happened in the المعركة of Badr, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah advanced, tearing his الطريق into the middle of the العدو ranks. This bold move of his created havoc and the العدو started to run helterskelter. He was going around fearlessly in the middle of the العدو as إذا الموت was of no consequence and there was absolutely no danger. His casual attitude frightened the Quraish. As soon as he came face to face with an العدو on horseback, the man would give الطريق to him. But there was one man among them who would stand in front of him to challenge him and try to attack him with his السيف. Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . would try to avoid him and retreat. But the man would insist on coming face to face to oppose him, and Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 would always give him free passage and get out of his الطريق. خلال the المعركة a stage came عندما the man had him trapped. There was no الطريق that Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 could turn to avoid him; he could neither move towards the front nor could he move back; all avenues were closed to him. Absolutely ensnared, he wأماced to attack him on the head with his السيف; his skull broke into two and in a moment he lay dead at his feet. Can you guess who this slain man could be? Doubtless the الابتلاء of faithfulness faced by Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah <% was extremely البالغ الأهمية, few people are forced into such a situation. عندما he realised that the dead man was his الأب he was stunned. The fact was that the man he had killed was to all appearances his الأب, but in reality it was the standard-bearer of الشر he had killed. Fuifilling this exemplary role he had made it clear that in the balance it was الله Almighty and His religion of الإسلام which outweighed all other considerations and even familial bonds. The المحبة of الله 3S takes priority and is superior to all other bonds. Every moment the objective is in sight and of paramount importance (the glory of الإسلام). إذا something comes in the الطريق it has to be chopped off; إذا a mountain is an obstacle it has to be surmounted; إذا there is a river in the الطريق the waves have to be cut through with powerful strokes to reach the friendly shores. The السيف of Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah did not chop off his الأب’s neck, but it chopped off the face of ’الكفر’ and one who associated others with the One True الله. This exploit of his pleased الله 38 لذا much that the following Aydt were revealed: “You (O محمد ﷺ 38) will not find any peopie who believe in الله and the Last Day, making friendship with those who oppose الله and His Messenger (محمد ﷺ 38), حتى وإن they were their fathers or their sons or their brothers or their kindred (people). For such He has written الإيمان in their القلوب, and strengthened them with Riih (proofs, light and true الهداية) from Himself. And He will admit them to Gardens (الجنة) under which rivers flow, to dwell therein (forever). الله is pleased with them and they with Him. They are the party of الله. Verily, it is the party of الله that will be the successful.” (59:22) What better fate could one ask for than praise for one’s deeds in the radiant الآيات revealed in the Noble القرآن الكريم? No doubt, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . received this enviable honour. As long as this الدنيا exists the Noble Qur’an will continue to be read, and the Mo’mineen' will give الحياة and الروح to their determination, الشجاعة and الإيمان.
